100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 17, 1926, the British government faced mounting pressure as widespread strikes continued to disrupt industries, particularly coal mining, leading to fears of a national crisis. In the United States, the Senate debated the Reed-Smoot Tariff, which could significantly impact international trade relations. Meanwhile, in Germany, political tensions heightened as the Weimar Republic grappled with economic instability and rising extremist movements.
Updated: Wednesday, June 17, 2026 at 09:41:29 AM CDT
